items.title: Boag's beer brand not for sale, but owner open to offers

children.content: The union representing Boag's brewery workers in Launceston wants its owner, Lion Australia, to sell the brand along with the brewery. But the company's response has left workers confused.

gallery.altText: A red brick building with a large Boags Brewery sign.

thumbnail.altText: A red brick building with a large Boags Brewery sign.

items.title: Infants now eligible for free meningococcal B vaccine in Tasmania

children.content: Vaccinations for the rare but potentially deadly meningococcal B disease are now free for all Tasmanian infants for the next four years.

image.altText: An adult hand holds a needle to the arm of a small child, who has a restful, neutral expression.

items.title: Renewed push to ban 'conversion practices' in Tasmania

children.content: Tasmania was the last Australian jurisdiction to decriminalise homosexuality, but since then it has gone on to pass some of the country's most inclusive legislation. There's one final frontier — conversion practices.

image.altText: A large rainbow flag is waved in the air.

items.title: Neil the seal’s antics make him popular, but there’s a big risk

children.content: Neil the seal has become one of Tasmania's biggest celebrities. But authorities are concerned his popularity could put him and the public at risk.

image.altText: A large elephant seal leans the front of his body on a timbe beach fence at night.

images.altText: Harry Kane and Jude Bellingham are face to face in celebration
A soccer player in white celebrates a goal by pulling at his shirt

children.content: Harry Kane strikes twice after half time as the Three Lions fight back to defeat DR Congo 2-1 and progress to the Round of 16. Follow live.

items.title: Kane saves England after DR Congo scare, Belgium faces Senegal

updates.title: FT: England pull off great escape in Atlanta

items.title: Labor MP says 'much more' to do on gambling ads

children.content: The Coalition, Greens and crossbench are also pushing for changes to the bill, which limits TV and radio ads during certain times and creates an opt-out feature for online platforms.

image.altText: An old white man with a balding head speaks wearing a suit

items.title: World's biggest porn site puts advertising block on Australian AI empire

children.content: Australian pornography website OurDream has had its advertising suspended by Pornhub after the ABC revealed OurDream's content could include child exploitation material.

image.altText: A montage of profile photos of characters with blurred faces and age estiamtes beside them.

items.title: Before and after images show widespread impact of Venezuelan quakes

children.content: Venezuela's devastating earthquakes flattened buildings and coated parts of the northern coast in dust and debris, satellite imagery shows.

gallery.altText: An aerial image of rows of residential homes and an ocean-side boat marina with significant earthquake damage.

thumbnail.altText: An aerial image of rows of residential homes and an ocean-side boat marina with significant earthquake damage.

items.title: Thai teen's ashes laid to rest by family after alleged murder

children.content: The family of a Thai teenager allegedly killed by an Australian man has placed her cremated ashes to rest at a local Buddhist temple as they seek justice for her murder.

items.title: Election review a 'hyper-partisan witch-hunt', Coalition says

children.content: A Coalition group has launched an extraordinary attack on one of parliament's top committees, slamming its election inquiry as biased.

image.altText: A crowd of people hold  up election propaganda at an intersection on a sunny day.

items.title: Calls for Football Australia to enforce restrictions on headers

children.content: With all eyes on the World Cup, Australian football's management of the risks associated with the practice of heading the ball, such as dementia and CTE, is in the spotlight.

gallery.altText: A soccer player in blue and orange heads a ball in front of trees in a park.

thumbnail.altText: A soccer player in blue and orange heads a ball in front of trees in a park.

avatar.alt: A woman with blonde hair holding a microphone.

items.title: Trump tries to claim a win on birthright citizenship Supreme Court ruling

children.content: While it's a blow to the US president, the highest court's narrow ruling may have emboldened efforts to overturn birthright citizenship through other avenues.

image.altText: A woman holds a sign saying "protect birthright citizenship" in front of the US supreme court

items.title: 'I can't breathe': Police who handcuffed Henry Nowak being investigated

children.content: Student Henry Nowak, 18, died while handcuffed by police in the English city of Southampton, after calling out "I can't breathe" and "I've been stabbed".

image.altText: The heads and hands of protesters, including one holding a white sign with a picture of a Caucasian teen.

items.title: What Matthew Flinders and George Bass can teach us about male friendship

children.content: Flinders circumnavigated and named Australia. Impressive stuff, but he also wrote many impassioned letters and lived a life full of crushes.

gallery.altText: A letter from 1800, cursive ink writing and faded paper

thumbnail.altText: A letter from 1800, cursive ink writing and faded paper

items.title: Torn urethras and Oscar nominations: Jackass's journey to infamy

children.content: For more than 25 years, prank franchise Jackass has been delighting teens and terrifying parents. Now the gang is heading to the cinemas for what could be their last ride.

image.altText: A bull gores a man dressed as a matador

items.title: How US soldiers helped inspire a religion on a remote island in Vanuatu

children.content: On Vanuatu's Tanna Island, I immerse myself in a culture that worships a shape-shifting spirit known as John Frum.

image.altText: Chief Moly stands in front of a wooden door, wearing a green Jesus shirt.

items.title: Village People lead singer Victor Willis dead at 74

children.content: The lead singer and a founding member of the Village People disco group has died at the age of 74.

gallery.altText: A man in a tight police outfit.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a tight police outfit.

items.title: Karl Stefanovic to be dumped from radio show, defends 'legitimate interview'

children.content: Karl Stefanovic will be dumped from his new radio show amid fallout from his interview with Tommy Robinson, the ABC understands.

items.title: Canada becomes latest country to join Eurovision Song Contest

children.content: While Canadian Céline Dion gave Switzerland its second Eurovision victory in 1988, next year will be the first time Canada competes in the song contest.

image.altText: DARA holds the trophy aloft as gold confetti falls from the ceiling

items.title: Pair behind dramatic flood rescue awarded for going 'beyond the call of duty'

children.content: A helicopter pilot and a police officer who performed a daring flood rescue in Katherine earlier this year have been awarded for their heroics as part of the Northern Territory's Territory Day awards.

image.altText: Man in high-vis sits on top of a 4WD vehicle half submerged in floodwater, holding a dog, as a helicopter hovers next to them.

gallery.altText: A male police officer, with a lot of medals pinned to his uniform, standing outside and smiling.

thumbnail.altText: A male police officer, with a lot of medals pinned to his uniform, standing outside and smiling.

avatar.alt: A journalist wears a button-up shirt and serious facial expression.

items.title: A decade ago Swans' CEO played in the little-known first Pride game

children.content: This Friday will mark 10 years of Sydney Swans Pride games for premiership points.

image.altText: Matthew Pavlich marched in this year's Mardi Gras for the Sydney Swans

items.title: Man charged over alleged people smuggling operation in Far North Queensland

children.content: The group allegedly came ashore from a boat at Pennefather Beach in the early hours of yesterday Tuesday morning.

image.altText: CCTV footage shows two men ordering from Weipa bakery.

items.title: Comedian says 'terrifying' roadside assistance encounter has easy solution

children.content: Comedian Laura Johnston calls for changes to the law after saying she was confronted by an NRMA contractor who could not provide identification, behaved aggressively and swore at her before leaving her stranded in remote NSW.

image.altText: A woman with dark hair and glasses looking into the camera, speaking.

items.title: Heroin overdose warning after multiple Victorians unknowingly take drug

children.content: One person has died and multiple people have overdosed across Victoria, leading to the discovery of heroin in substances thought to be methamphetamine or cocaine.

image.altText: White power drug, sprinkled on reflective surface, with ziplock plastic bag.

items.title: Who is Maya Joint, Australian conqueror of Serena at Wimbledon?

children.content: Maya Joint was born in April 2006 when Serena Williams had already won seven Grand Slam singles titles. So who is the 20-year-old who just defeated a tennis legend in her return to the All England Club?

gallery.altText: A red-headed female tennis player in white pokes tongue out on Wimbledon centre court

thumbnail.altText: A red-headed female tennis player in white pokes tongue out on Wimbledon centre court

avatar.alt: A middle-aged man in a grey suit and blue shirt smiles and looks at camera.
